concerns.text: Task T029 explicitly forbids fallback to condition-level aggregation ('NO FALLBACK'), but the plan (Phase 1 T016) and spec (Edge Cases) define a fallback mechanism for pairing failures. This creates a coverage gap where the plan's defined recovery path has no corresponding task to implement it, leaving the system unable to satisfy the plan's contingency logic.

Task T043 hardcodes PCA reduction to 'top 50 components' regardless of feature count. The plan (Phase 2 T025) and spec (FR-004/US-2) define PCA as conditional (only if p > 2n) and do not specify a fixed component count. There is no task implementing the conditional logic (p > 2n check) or the dynamic calculation of components, creating a gap between the plan's conditional requirement and the task's static implementation.

Task T036 adds a strict 'NO regulatory genes' constraint not present in the spec (FR-004) or plan (Phase 2 T024). While the task covers the KEGG mapping requirement, the added constraint alters the scope of the feature set without a corresponding requirement in the spec or plan to justify this exclusion, potentially violating the plan's defined feature selection criteria.

Task T006 requires aborting if n < 28 based on power analysis. While the plan (Phase 0 T009) mentions this, the spec (FR-009) focuses on the 95% pairing rate threshold for aborting. There is no explicit FR/SC in the spec mandating the n=28 abort condition, leaving this task's critical abort logic unsupported by a primary requirement in the spec.

Task T056 creates the cross-species model but fails to explicitly state that SC-001 (r≥0.5) and SC-002 (p≤0.05) do not apply to this exploratory model. The plan (Phase 3 T039) and spec (SC-001/SC-002) define these success criteria for the primary models. Without explicit exclusion in the task, the implementer cannot verify if the success criteria apply, creating ambiguity in the coverage of SC-001/SC-002 for this specific model variant.

Task T005 creates a power analysis utility with an '80% power' parameter. The plan (Phase 0 T009) and spec (Assumptions) use '[deferred]' for the power value and do not define 80% as a requirement. There is no FR/SC or plan element mandating 80% power, leaving this specific parameter unsupported by the source documents.
